methodology: 25-question gold set · streaming · TTFT captured · one-shot, no repair, no retry · JSON mode · temp 0 · max_tokens 1024 — same scored protocol as data/baseten_sweep.json. Inter-call pacing raised to 5.0s (vs Baseten's 1.5s) to clear Fireworks shared-serverless 429s so accuracy reflects the model.

traces.question: What are the top 5 best-selling genres by total sales?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT g.Name AS Genre, SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) AS TotalSales FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY g.GenreId, g.Name ORDER BY TotalSales DESC LIMIT 5","rationale":"Joins Genre to Track to InvoiceLine, sums UnitPrice*Quantity as total sales per genre, and returns the top 5 by descending sales."}
traces.sql: SELECT g.Name AS Genre, SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) AS TotalSales FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY g.GenreId, g.Name ORDER BY TotalSales DESC LIMIT 5

traces.question: List all albums by the artist 'AC/DC'.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT Title FROM Album JOIN Artist ON Album.ArtistId = Artist.ArtistId WHERE Artist.Name = 'AC/DC'","rationale":"Join Album with Artist to filter by AC/DC and return their album titles."}
traces.sql: SELECT Title FROM Album JOIN Artist ON Album.ArtistId = Artist.ArtistId WHERE Artist.Name = 'AC/DC'

rows.Title: For Those About To Rock We Salute You

traces.question: What are the names and email addresses of customers from Brazil?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT FirstName, LastName, Email FROM Customer WHERE Country = 'Brazil'","rationale":"Selects the first name, last name, and email of all customers whose country is Brazil."}
traces.sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, Email FROM Customer WHERE Country = 'Brazil'

traces.question: What is the most popular media type based on number of tracks?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT mt.Name AS MediaTypeName, COUNT(t.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM MediaType mt JOIN Track t ON mt.MediaTypeId = t.MediaTypeId GROUP BY mt.MediaTypeId, mt.Name ORDER BY TrackCount DESC LIMIT 1","rationale":"Count tracks per media type and return the one with the highest count."}
traces.sql: SELECT mt.Name AS MediaTypeName, COUNT(t.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM MediaType mt JOIN Track t ON mt.MediaTypeId = t.MediaTypeId GROUP BY mt.MediaTypeId, mt.Name ORDER BY TrackCount DESC LIMIT 1

traces.question: Which employee has the most customers assigned to them?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS EmployeeName, COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId ORDER BY CustomerCount DESC LIMIT 1","rationale":"Joins Employee and Customer on SupportRepId, counts customers per employee, and returns the one with the highest count."}
traces.sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS EmployeeName, COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId ORDER BY CustomerCount DESC LIMIT 1

traces.question: How many tracks are there in each playlist?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT p.Name AS PlaylistName, COUNT(pt.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Playlist p JOIN PlaylistTrack pt ON p.PlaylistId = pt.PlaylistId GROUP BY p.PlaylistId, p.Name ORDER BY TrackCount DESC","rationale":"Join Playlist with PlaylistTrack to count tracks per playlist, grouping by playlist and ordering by count descending."}
traces.sql: SELECT p.Name AS PlaylistName, COUNT(pt.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Playlist p JOIN PlaylistTrack pt ON p.PlaylistId = pt.PlaylistId GROUP BY p.PlaylistId, p.Name ORDER BY TrackCount DESC

traces.question: What is the total revenue generated in the year 2021?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT SUM(Total) AS TotalRevenue FROM Invoice WHERE strftime('%Y', InvoiceDate) = '2021'","rationale":"Filters invoices to 2021 using strftime and sums the Total column to get revenue."}
traces.sql: SELECT SUM(Total) AS TotalRevenue FROM Invoice WHERE strftime('%Y', InvoiceDate) = '2021'

traces.question: What is the average invoice total for each billing country, ordered from highest to lowest?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT BillingCountry, AVG(Total) AS AverageInvoiceTotal FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry ORDER BY AverageInvoiceTotal DESC","rationale":"Calculate the average invoice total per billing country by grouping on BillingCountry and ordering the results descending by the computed average."}
traces.sql: SELECT BillingCountry, AVG(Total) AS AverageInvoiceTotal FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry ORDER BY AverageInvoiceTotal DESC

traces.question: For each customer, what is their total spending and how does it rank compared to other customers? Show the top 5 customers by spending.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS CustomerName, SUM(i.Total) AS TotalSpending, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(i.Total) DESC) AS SpendingRank FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName ORDER BY TotalSpending DESC LIMIT 5","rationale":"Join Customer and Invoice to sum each customer's invoice totals, then use RANK() to compare spending across customers and limit to the top 5."}
traces.sql: SELECT c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS CustomerName, SUM(i.Total) AS TotalSpending, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(i.Total) DESC) AS SpendingRank FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName ORDER BY TotalSpending DESC LIMIT 5

traces.question: Which artists have tracks in more than 3 different genres? List the artist name and the number of distinct genres.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT ar.Name AS ArtistName, COUNT(DISTINCT t.GenreId) AS GenreCount FROM Artist ar JOIN Album al ON ar.ArtistId = al.ArtistId JOIN Track t ON al.AlbumId = t.AlbumId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.GenreId) > 3","rationale":"Join Artist to Album to Track, group by artist, count distinct genres per artist, and filter for those with more than 3 genres."}
traces.sql: SELECT ar.Name AS ArtistName, COUNT(DISTINCT t.GenreId) AS GenreCount FROM Artist ar JOIN Album al ON ar.ArtistId = al.ArtistId JOIN Track t ON al.AlbumId = t.AlbumId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.GenreId) > 3

traces.question: How many tracks are longer than the average track length (in milliseconds)?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track WHERE Milliseconds > (SELECT AVG(Milliseconds) FROM Track)","rationale":"Count tracks whose Milliseconds exceed the average Milliseconds across all tracks."}
traces.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track WHERE Milliseconds > (SELECT AVG(Milliseconds) FROM Track)

traces.question: Bucket tracks by length: short (< 3 min), medium (3–5 min), long (> 5 min). How many tracks are in each bucket?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT CASE WHEN Milliseconds < 180000 THEN 'short' WHEN Milliseconds BETWEEN 180000 AND 300000 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'long' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track GROUP BY bucket","rationale":"Classify tracks into length buckets using CASE on Milliseconds, then count tracks per bucket."}
traces.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN Milliseconds < 180000 THEN 'short' WHEN Milliseconds BETWEEN 180000 AND 300000 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'long' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track GROUP BY bucket

traces.question: Which employees do not report to a manager? Show their full name and title.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT FirstName ||' '|| LastName AS full_name, Title FROM Employee WHERE ReportsTo IS NULL","rationale":"Select employees with no manager by filtering where ReportsTo is NULL, concatenating first and last names as full name."}
traces.sql: SELECT FirstName ||' '|| LastName AS full_name, Title FROM Employee WHERE ReportsTo IS NULL

traces.question: How many invoices were issued in the first quarter of 2022 (January through March)?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount FROM Invoice WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', InvoiceDate) BETWEEN '2022-01' AND '2022-03'","rationale":"Count invoices where the year-month falls within January through March 2022 using strftime for date filtering."}
traces.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount FROM Invoice WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', InvoiceDate) BETWEEN '2022-01' AND '2022-03'

traces.question: List each employee with their manager's name. Include employees who have no manager (show the manager column as NULL).
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT e.FirstName ||' '|| e.LastName AS EmployeeName, m.FirstName ||' '|| m.LastName AS ManagerName FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Employee m ON e.ReportsTo = m.EmployeeId","rationale":"Uses a self-join with LEFT JOIN to include all employees and show their manager's name or NULL when they have no manager."}
traces.sql: SELECT e.FirstName ||' '|| e.LastName AS EmployeeName, m.FirstName ||' '|| m.LastName AS ManagerName FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Employee m ON e.ReportsTo = m.EmployeeId

traces.question: How many distinct countries do we have customers in?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Country) FROM Customer","rationale":"Counts the number of unique country values in the Customer table."}
traces.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Country) FROM Customer

traces.question: List every distinct country that appears either as a customer's country or as an invoice's billing country. Order alphabetically.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT Country FROM Customer UNION SELECT BillingCountry AS Country FROM Invoice ORDER BY Country","rationale":"UNION combines distinct countries from both Customer.Country and Invoice.BillingCountry, and ORDER BY sorts the result alphabetically."}
traces.sql: SELECT Country FROM Customer UNION SELECT BillingCountry AS Country FROM Invoice ORDER BY Country

traces.question: What is the average number of tracks per album, across all albums?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT AlbumId) AS avg_tracks_per_album FROM Track","rationale":"Counts all tracks and divides by the number of distinct albums to get the average tracks per album."}
traces.sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT AlbumId) AS avg_tracks_per_album FROM Track

traces.question: List all employees and the number of customers they support. Show 0 for employees who support no customers. Order by customer count descending, then by employee name.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT e.FirstName ||' '|| e.LastName AS EmployeeName, COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, e.FirstName, e.LastName ORDER BY CustomerCount DESC, EmployeeName","rationale":"LEFT JOIN ensures employees with no customers show 0, and COUNT on CustomerId handles the zero count correctly."}
traces.sql: SELECT e.FirstName ||' '|| e.LastName AS EmployeeName, COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, e.FirstName, e.LastName ORDER BY CustomerCount DESC, EmployeeName

traces.question: How many tracks have the word 'love' (case-insensitive) anywhere in their name?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track WHERE LOWER(Name) LIKE '%love%'","rationale":"Count tracks where the lowercase name contains 'love' anywhere."}
traces.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Track WHERE LOWER(Name) LIKE '%love%'

traces.question: What is the longest track in each genre? Show the genre name, the track name, and the length in milliseconds. Order by genre name.
traces.raw_content:
{"sql":"SELECT g.Name AS GenreName, t.Name AS TrackName, t.Milliseconds AS Length
FROM Genre g
JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId
WHERE t.Milliseconds = (
    SELECT MAX(Milliseconds)
    FROM Track
    WHERE GenreId = g.GenreId
)
ORDER BY g.Name","rationale":"For each genre, find the track with maximum milliseconds using a correlated subquery, then return genre name, track name, and length ordered alphabetically by genre."}
traces.sql:
SELECT g.Name AS GenreName, t.Name AS TrackName, t.Milliseconds AS Length
FROM Genre g
JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId
WHERE t.Milliseconds = (
    SELECT MAX(Milliseconds)
    FROM Track
    WHERE GenreId = g.GenreId
)
ORDER BY g.Name

traces.question: Bucket invoices by total: small (< $5), medium ($5–$10), large (> $10). Show the count of invoices in each bucket.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT CASE WHEN Total < 5 THEN 'small' WHEN Total BETWEEN 5 AND 10 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM Invoice GROUP BY bucket","rationale":"Use a CASE expression to classify each invoice by its Total into three buckets, then group by that bucket and count invoices in each."}
traces.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN Total < 5 THEN 'small' WHEN Total BETWEEN 5 AND 10 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM Invoice GROUP BY bucket

traces.question: What was the total revenue in the last 6 months of available invoice data?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT SUM(Total) AS TotalRevenue FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceDate >= date((SELECT MAX(InvoiceDate) FROM Invoice), '-6 months')","rationale":"Calculate the sum of invoice totals for the 6-month period ending at the latest invoice date in the dataset."}
traces.sql: SELECT SUM(Total) AS TotalRevenue FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceDate >= date((SELECT MAX(InvoiceDate) FROM Invoice), '-6 months')

traces.question: Who is the top-spending customer for each support rep? Show the rep's name, the customer's name, and the customer's total spending. Order by total spending descending.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS RepName, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS CustomerName, SUM(i.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, c.CustomerId HAVING SUM(i.Total) = (SELECT SUM(i2.Total) FROM Customer c2 JOIN Invoice i2 ON c2.CustomerId = i2.CustomerId WHERE c2.SupportRepId = e.EmployeeId GROUP BY c2.CustomerId ORDER BY SUM(i2.Total) DESC LIMIT 1) ORDER
traces.sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS RepName, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS CustomerName, SUM(i.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, c.CustomerId HAVING SUM(i.Total) = (SELECT SUM(i2.Total) FROM Customer c2 JOIN Invoice i2 ON c2.CustomerId = i2.CustomerId WHERE c2.SupportRepId = e.EmployeeId GROUP BY c2.CustomerId ORDER BY SUM(i2.Total) DESC LIMIT 1) ORDER BY TotalSpending DESC

traces.question: Which customers in cities starting with 'S' have more than 1 invoice? Show their name, city, and invoice count. Order by invoice count descending, then by customer name.

traces.sql: SELECT c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS Name, c.City, COUNT(i.InvoiceId) AS InvoiceCount FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.City LIKE 'S%' GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.City HAVING COUNT(i.InvoiceId) > 1 ORDER BY InvoiceCount DESC, Name

traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT CASE WHEN Total < 5 THEN 'small' WHEN Total <= 10 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM Invoice GROUP BY bucket","rationale":"Uses a CASE expression to categorize invoices by total amount into three buckets, then counts invoices in each group."}
traces.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN Total < 5 THEN 'small' WHEN Total <= 10 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM Invoice GROUP BY bucket

traces.question: What was the total revenue in the last 6 months of available invoice data?
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT SUM(Total) AS TotalRevenue FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceDate >= (SELECT MAX(InvoiceDate) FROM Invoice) - INTERVAL '6 months'","rationale":"Calculate total revenue by summing Invoice.Total for invoices dated within 6 months of the most recent invoice date."}

traces.question: Who is the top-spending customer for each support rep? Show the rep's name, the customer's name, and the customer's total spending. Order by total spending descending.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS RepName, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS CustomerName, SUM(i.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, c.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpending DESC","rationale":"Joins Employee to Customer to Invoice, groups by rep and customer to calculate total spending per customer, and orders by spending descending to show top spenders first."}
traces.sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS RepName, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS CustomerName, SUM(i.Total) AS TotalSpending FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, c.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpending DESC

traces.question: Which customers in cities starting with 'S' have more than 1 invoice? Show their name, city, and invoice count. Order by invoice count descending, then by customer name.
traces.raw_content: {"sql":"SELECT c.FirstName ||' '|| c.LastName AS name, c.City, COUNT(i.InvoiceId) AS invoice_count FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.City LIKE 'S%' GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.City HAVING COUNT(i.InvoiceId) > 1 ORDER BY invoice_count DESC, name","rationale":"Filters customers in cities starting with 'S', groups by customer to count invoices, keeps only those with more than 1 invoice, and orders by count descending then name."}
traces.sql: SELECT c.FirstName ||' '|| c.LastName AS name, c.City, COUNT(i.InvoiceId) AS invoice_count FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.City LIKE 'S%' GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.City HAVING COUNT(i.InvoiceId) > 1 ORDER BY invoice_count DESC, name

traces.question: What is the longest track in each genre? Show the genre name, the track name, and the length in milliseconds. Order by genre name.
traces.raw_content:
{
"sql": "WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT 
    g.Name AS GenreName,
    t.Name AS TrackName,
    t.Milliseconds,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY t.GenreId ORDER BY t.Milliseconds DESC) AS rn
  FROM Track t
  JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId
)
SELECT GenreName, TrackName, Milliseconds
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY GenreName",
"rationale": "Use ROW_NUMBER() partitioned by GenreId to rank tracks by descending length, then select the top-ranked track per genre, joining Genre for t
traces.sql:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT 
    g.Name AS GenreName,
    t.Name AS TrackName,
    t.Milliseconds,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY t.GenreId ORDER BY t.Milliseconds DESC) AS rn
  FROM Track t
  JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId
)
SELECT GenreName, TrackName, Milliseconds
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY GenreName

traces.question: Who is the top-spending customer for each support rep? Show the rep's name, the customer's name, and the customer's total spending. Order by total spending descending.
traces.raw_content:
{
"sql": "WITH customer_spending AS (
    SELECT c.SupportRepId, c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, SUM(i.Total) AS total_spent
    FROM Customer c
    JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
    GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.SupportRepId, c.FirstName, c.LastName
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY SupportRepId ORDER BY total_spent DESC) AS rn
    FROM customer_spending
)
SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS rep_name,
       r.FirstName
traces.sql:
WITH customer_spending AS (
    SELECT c.SupportRepId, c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, SUM(i.Total) AS total_spent
    FROM Customer c
    JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
    GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.SupportRepId, c.FirstName, c.LastName
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY SupportRepId ORDER BY total_spent DESC) AS rn
    FROM customer_spending
)
SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS rep_name,
       r.FirstName || ' ' || r.LastName AS customer_name,
       r.total_spent
FROM ranked r
JOIN Employee e ON r.SupportRepId = e.EmployeeId
WHERE r.rn = 1
ORDER BY r.total_spent DESC

traces.question: List each employee with their manager's name. Include employees who have no manager (show the manager column as NULL).
How many distinct countries do we have customers in?
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Country) FROM Customer", "rationale": "Count distinct Country values from the Customer table to get the number of unique countries."}
traces.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Country) FROM Customer

traces.question: List every distinct country that appears either as a customer's country or as an invoice's billing country. Order alphabetically.
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT Country FROM Customer UNION SELECT BillingCountry FROM Invoice ORDER BY Country", "rationale": "Combines distinct countries from both the Customer and Invoice tables using UNION, then orders them alphabetically."}
traces.sql: SELECT Country FROM Customer UNION SELECT BillingCountry FROM Invoice ORDER BY Country

traces.question: What is the average number of tracks per album, across all albums?
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT AVG(track_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track GROUP BY AlbumId)", "rationale": "This query counts tracks per album and then calculates the average of those counts."}
traces.sql: SELECT AVG(track_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track GROUP BY AlbumId)

traces.question: List all employees and the number of customers they support. Show 0 for employees who support no customers. Order by customer count descending, then by employee name.
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS EmployeeName, COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, e.FirstName, e.LastName ORDER BY CustomerCount DESC, EmployeeName", "rationale": "Left join employees with customers on support rep relationship, count customers per employee (NULLs count as 0), sort by count descending then name."}
traces.sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS EmployeeName, COUNT(c.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, e.FirstName, e.LastName ORDER BY CustomerCount DESC, EmployeeName

traces.question: How many tracks have the word 'love' (case-insensitive) anywhere in their name?
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track WHERE Name LIKE '%love%'", "rationale": "Counts all tracks where the name contains 'love' anywhere, using LIKE with wildcards for case-insensitive matching."}
traces.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track WHERE Name LIKE '%love%'

traces.question: What is the longest track in each genre? Show the genre name, the track name, and the length in milliseconds. Order by genre name.
traces.sql: SELECT g.Name, t.Name, t.Milliseconds FROM Track t JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId WHERE t.Milliseconds = (SELECT MAX(t2.Milliseconds) FROM Track t2 WHERE t2.GenreId = t.GenreId) ORDER BY g.Name

rows.Name: Homecoming / The Death Of St. Jimmy / East 12th St. / Nobody Likes You / Rock And Roll Girlfriend / We're Coming Home Again
Adagio for Strings from the String Quartet, Op. 11
Die Zauberflöte, K.620: "Der Hölle Rache Kocht in Meinem Herze"

traces.question: Bucket invoices by total: small (< $5), medium ($5–$10), large (> $10). Show the count of invoices in each bucket.
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT CASE WHEN Total < 5 THEN 'small' WHEN Total <= 10 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM Invoice GROUP BY bucket ORDER BY CASE bucket WHEN 'small' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 WHEN 'large' THEN 3 END", "rationale": "Categorize invoices into small/medium/large buckets using a CASE expression on Total, then count invoices per bucket and order logically."}
traces.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN Total < 5 THEN 'small' WHEN Total <= 10 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'large' END AS bucket, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM Invoice GROUP BY bucket ORDER BY CASE bucket WHEN 'small' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 WHEN 'large' THEN 3 END

traces.question: What was the total revenue in the last 6 months of available invoice data?
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT SUM(Total) AS total_revenue FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceDate >= date((SELECT MAX(InvoiceDate) FROM Invoice), '-6 months')", "rationale": "Sums the Total column from invoices in the last 6 months relative to the most recent invoice date."}
traces.sql: SELECT SUM(Total) AS total_revenue FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceDate >= date((SELECT MAX(InvoiceDate) FROM Invoice), '-6 months')

traces.question: Who is the top-spending customer for each support rep? Show the rep's name, the customer's name, and the customer's total spending. Order by total spending descending.
Which customers in cities starting with 'S' have more than 1 invoice? Show their name, city, and invoice count. Order by invoice count descending, then by customer name.
traces.raw_content: {"sql": "SELECT c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS Name, c.City, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.City LIKE 'S%' GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.City HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName ASC", "rationale": "Joins Customer with Invoice, filters cities starting with S, groups by customer to count invoices, filters for count > 1, and orders by invoice count desc then name asc."}
traces.sql: SELECT c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName AS Name, c.City, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.City LIKE 'S%' GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.City HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName ASC
